seo.title: Edge Deployment tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Edge Deployment tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of infrastructure, databases, and tooling specifically designed for edge runtimes like V8 isolates and WASM. This resource focuses on low-latency execution and tools compatible with restricted APIs found in edge environments.

entries.name: Cloudflare Workers
entries.description: Serverless execution environment based on V8 isolates, providing low-latency execution at over 300 global data centers.
pros: Built-in Key-Value storage and Cron triggers
cons: Strict 128MB memory limit on standard plan
No support for Node.js built-in modules like 'fs' or 'net'

entries.name: Deno Deploy
entries.description: Distributed system that runs JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly at the edge with native support for Web APIs.
pros: Native TypeScript support without build steps
No vendor lock-in via Deno CLI compatibility
cons: Smaller ecosystem compared to Node.js/npm
Limited third-party integration compared to Cloudflare

entries.name: Turso
entries.description: Edge-hosted database based on libSQL, designed for low-latency access from edge functions via HTTP.
cons: Limited to SQL-based workloads
Relatively new ecosystem

entries.name: Upstash
entries.description: Serverless Redis, Kafka, and Vector database services with per-request pricing and HTTP-based access.
pros: HTTP API bypasses TCP connection limits in edge functions
cons: Higher latency than local in-memory cache
Strict rate limits on free tier

entries.name: Hono
entries.description: Small, fast web framework built for edge runtimes like Cloudflare Workers, Fastly, and Deno.
pros: Built-in middleware for JWT, CORS, and Validation
cons: Minimalist approach may require manual setup for complex apps
Documentation focused primarily on TypeScript

entries.name: Wrangler
entries.description: The official CLI for managing, testing, and deploying Cloudflare Workers and Pages.
cons: Can be complex to configure for monorepos
Specific to the Cloudflare ecosystem

entries.name: Fastly Compute
entries.description: WASM-based edge computing platform offering high performance and security isolation.
pros: Supports Rust, Go, and JavaScript via WASM
No cold starts due to pre-initialized instances
cons: Higher barrier to entry for non-WASM languages
Pricing is geared toward enterprise usage

entries.name: PlanetScale
entries.description: Serverless MySQL database platform with horizontal scaling and branching workflows.
pros: Connection string compatibility with edge adapters
cons: Removed free tier recently
No foreign key constraint support in some configurations

entries.name: Miniflare
entries.description: A fully-featured local simulator for Cloudflare Workers, including KV, D1, and R2 support.
pros: Simulates persistence for KV and D1
Easy integration with Jest or Vitest
cons: Minor discrepancies from production runtime behavior
Memory usage can spike with large simulated datasets

entries.name: Logflare
entries.description: Cloud-native logging and analytics platform optimized for Cloudflare Workers and Vercel.
cons: UI can be less intuitive than Datadog
Limited advanced alerting on lower tiers

entries.name: Itty Router
entries.description: An ultra-lightweight (approx 450 bytes) router specifically for edge functions.
cons: Lacks built-in middleware for common tasks like CORS
Manual error handling required

entries.name: Prisma Accelerate
entries.description: A global database cache and connection pooler designed for serverless and edge functions.
pros: Solves connection pooling issues at the edge
Global edge caching for database queries
Works with existing Prisma ORM workflows
cons: Adds an extra network hop to the request
Requires using Prisma as your ORM
